reasoning: Between October 30 (market odds ~7%) and November 9, 2025, the 49ers suffered a setback as starting QB Brock Purdy missed time with a turf toe injury, forcing Mac Jones to start. On November 9, they endured a decisive 42-26 divisional loss to the Los Angeles Rams. This dropped their record to 6-4, leaving them trailing the 7-2 Rams in the NFC West and revealing massive vulnerabilities on defense. While they are still strong playoff contenders given their easy remaining schedule, the uncertainty surrounding Purdy's health and the likelihood of having to take a wildcard route significantly diminish their Super Bowl chances. Adjusting down from the October 30 anchor of 0.07, a 4.1% probability appropriately reflects the downgrade caused by their Week 10 loss and QB situation.
